The '''Galacian Gubernatorial election of 44 BBY''' was the first time a [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] of [[Gala]] was chosen democratically. Prior to [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]], the [[Beju-Tallah dynasty]] ruled Gala. However, [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Veda (Galacian)|Veda]] decided to change the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] to a democracy, in order to stem off widespread corruption and popular unrest.<ref name="TMC" />

There were three candidates. [[Deca Brun]] received widespread support, due his promises of reform and prosperity. [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Beju]], son of Queen Veda, also ran for governor. He believed that he had a right to rule, as the rightful heir to the throne, and opposed the election. The final candidate was [[Wila Prammi]], a former underminister.<ref name="TMC" />

Shortly before the election, [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] discovered that Deca Brun received large campaign contributions from [[Offworld Mining Corporation]]. In return, Offworld was to receive extensive mining rights, which would have caused extensive environmental damage. Meanwhile, Beju learned that he was not the true heir to the Galacian throne; [[Elan Tallah|Elan]], the leader of the hill people, was actually his older half-sister. Beju withdrew from the election, and supported Prammi instead. He also informed the electorate about Deca Brun's unscrupulous deal. Thus, Wila Prammi won by an overwhelming margin, becoming the first governor of Gala.<ref name="TMC" />
